title: Predicting Labels Per Token
concept: Sequence labeling assigns a category label to EVERY word in a text (e.g. labeling nouns vs verbs, or tagging named entities).

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of sequence labeling?

options.text: Assign labels to individual words in a sentence
Translate a sentence to another language
Generate an image from a text description

exercises.explain: Sequence labeling is all about assigning tags (labels) to individual tokens (words) in a sequence of text.

cards.title: Smart Highlights
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how your phone automatically highlights names, dates, or addresses in a text message? It doesn't just guess the topic of the text—it scans every single word to find the important bits.

cards.title: What is Sequence Labeling?
cards.body: Sequence labeling is a task where we look at a sentence and assign a specific label (or tag) to every single word (or token). Instead of classifying the whole sentence as 'happy' or 'sad', we analyze it word-by-word.

cards.title: The Fridge Organizer
cards.body: Imagine you are labeling leftovers in the fridge. Instead of putting one big tag on the whole box that says 'Dinner', you put a sticker on every single item inside: 'Pizza' on the slice, 'Apple' on the fruit, and 'Soda' on the can. Every single item gets its own individual tag!

cards.title: Tagging a Sentence
cards.body:
Let's look at the sentence: 'Alice visited Paris.' 

Using sequence labeling, a smart AI tags each word:
• Alice ➔ PERSON
• visited ➔ ACTION
• Paris ➔ LOCATION

cards.title: Myth: It's Just for Grammar
cards.body: Some people think sequence labeling only works for finding nouns and verbs. Actually, it can label anything you want! You can use it to find food ingredients in recipes, product names in tweets, or medical terms in doctor notes.

cards.title: Text vs. Token Labeling
cards.body: See how sequence labeling differs from classifying the whole text as one category.

left.text: Labels the whole text: 'I love Tokyo' -> 'Positive'

exercises.prompt: What makes sequence labeling different from normal text classification?

options.text: It assigns a label to every single word, not just the whole text.
It only works on numbers and never on text or words.
It deletes words that are not useful in the sentence.
It translates the entire sentence into another language.

exercises.explain: Unlike text classification which gives one label to the whole text, sequence labeling makes a prediction for every single word.

exercises.prompt: True or False: In sequence labeling, the AI only outputs one label for the entire sentence.
exercises.explain: Sequence labeling models output a tag for every token in the sequence. If you input 5 words, you get 5 labels back.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of sequence labeling.
exercises.sentence: In sequence labeling, the goal is to predict a specific [blank] for every single token in the text.
exercises.explain: Sequence labeling requires the model to assign a specific label to every single token (word) in the sequence.

exercises.prompt: Which AI output shows a sequence labeling task?

options.text: AI A: 'Sentence: I love pizza. -> Label: Positive'
AI B: 'Sentence: I love pizza. -> Labels: I (PRONOUN), love (VERB), pizza (NOUN)'
Both AI A and AI B are doing sequence labeling.

exercises.explain: AI B is doing sequence labeling by tagger-indexing every single token in the input sentence. AI A is doing overall sentiment classification.

exercises.prompt: Which of these tasks is the best example of Sequence Labeling in action?

options.text: Classifying if a customer review is positive or negative overall
Predicting the next word that will appear in a search bar
Labeling each word in a resume as 'Name', 'Skill', or 'Job Title'
Sorting incoming support emails into spam and not spam folders

exercises.explain: In sequence labeling, every single word (token) in the input text must be assigned a label. Identifying if the whole email is spam is text classification, not sequence labeling.
